title: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese

seo.title: Palak Paneer Recipe: Easy Indian Spinach & Paneer Curry
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Palak Paneer with our easy, authentic Indian recipe. This creamy spinach and cottage cheese curry is perfect for a healthy vegetarian meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Palak Paneer Recipe: Indian Spinach & Cottage Cheese Curry
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Palak Paneer
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy green Palak Paneer with cubes of cottage cheese, garnished with fresh cream and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Our easy Palak Paneer recipe brings the authentic taste of Indian spinach and cottage cheese curry to your home.

description: Palak Paneer is a classic Indian vegetarian dish featuring fresh spinach puree and soft cubes of paneer cottage cheese. This wholesome curry boasts a vibrant green color and a rich, creamy texture, subtly spiced with aromatic Indian masalas. It offers a delightful balance of earthy spinach and mild paneer, making it a beloved comfort food across India.

ingredients.notes: slit or finely chopped, adjust to taste

instructions.title: Prepare Spinach and Paneer
instructions.text: Wash the spinach thoroughly. Blanch it in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then immediately transfer to ice-cold water to retain its vibrant green color. Drain and blend into a smooth puree. Separately, heat a little oil in a pan and lightly pan-fry the paneer cubes until golden on all sides, then set aside. Alternatively, you can use raw paneer.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat ghee or oil in a large pan or kadai over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Then add the finely chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent and light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook Ginger, Garlic, and Chili
instructions.text: Add the ginger-garlic paste and chopped green chilies to the pan. Sauté for another 1-2 minutes until the raw aroma disappears.

instructions.title: Add Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree and cook until the oil starts to separate from the mixture, about 5-8 minutes. Now, add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for another minute.

instructions.title: Combine with Spinach Puree
instructions.text: Pour the spinach puree into the pan and mix thoroughly with the masala. Add about 1/2 cup of water to adjust the consistency. Bring it to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Finish with Paneer and Garam Masala
instructions.text: Add the lightly fried paneer cubes to the simmering spinach gravy. Sprinkle with garam masala and crushed Kasuri Methi, if using. Mix gently and cook for just 2-3 minutes more to allow the paneer to absorb the flavors. Do not overcook the paneer as it can become rubbery.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with a swirl of fresh cream, if desired, and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro. Serve hot with naan, roti, paratha, or steamed basmati rice.

tips: For the brightest green spinach, always blanch it quickly in boiling water and then immediately shock it in ice water. This halts the cooking process and preserves the vibrant color.
You can either use raw paneer cubes or lightly pan-fry them in a little ghee until golden. Frying adds a slightly crisp exterior and prevents the paneer from crumbling in the curry.

Adjust the consistency of your Palak Paneer by adding more hot water or a splash of milk if you prefer a thinner gravy. For extra richness, a touch of fresh cream or cashew paste can be added at the end.
article:
Palak Paneer: A Verdant Delight of Indian Cuisine

Few dishes encapsulate the vibrant flavors and wholesome goodness of Indian vegetarian cooking quite like Palak Paneer. This beloved classic, a staple in homes and restaurants across India and beyond, offers a delightful fusion of earthy spinach and creamy Indian cottage cheese. It’s a dish that not only tantalizes the taste buds but also provides a nutritious boost, making it a perfect choice for health-conscious foodies and comfort food seekers alike. If you're looking to explore the rich tapestry of Indian cuisine, learning to make Palak Paneer is an excellent place to start.

## What is Palak Paneer?

At its heart, Palak Paneer is a simple yet profoundly satisfying dish. The name itself reveals its core components: "Palak" refers to spinach, and "Paneer" is the traditional Indian cheese, akin to cottage cheese but firmer and non-melting. The dish typically features blanched and puréed spinach cooked with a medley of aromatic spices, garlic, ginger, and sometimes onions and tomatoes, into a thick, vibrant green gravy. Cubes of soft paneer are then gently folded into this luscious sauce, absorbing the flavors and adding a delightful textural contrast.

The beauty of Palak Paneer lies in its balanced profile. It’s mildly spiced, allowing the natural flavors of the spinach and paneer to shine through, complemented by the warmth of cumin, coriander, and a hint of garam masala. It’s naturally gluten-free and packed with nutrients. Spinach provides iron, vitamins A and K, while paneer is an excellent source of protein and calcium, making this dish a powerhouse of nutrition, especially for vegetarians.

## Ingredients for Palak Paneer

Creating an authentic and delicious Palak Paneer requires a handful of fresh ingredients and common Indian spices. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **Paneer:** 250-300 grams (about 9-10 ounces) – fresh, firm Indian cottage cheese, cut into 1-inch cubes.
*   **Spinach:** 500 grams (about 1 pound) – fresh spinach leaves, washed thoroughly.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 medium, finely chopped or puréed (optional, for a slight tang).
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon (freshly made is best).
*   **Green Chillies:** 1-2, slit or finely chopped (adjust to your spice preference).
*   **Ghee or Oil:** 2 tablespoons (ghee adds a richer, authentic flavor).
*   **Cumin Seeds:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Coriander Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Red Chilli Powder:** 1/2 teaspoon (adjust to your spice preference).
*   **Garam Masala:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Water or Cream:** 1/4 to 1/2 cup (for adjusting consistency and richness).
*   **Fresh Cream (for garnish):** 1 tablespoon (optional).
*   **Fresh Coriander Leaves (for garnish):** Chopped (optional).

## Step-by-Step Palak Paneer Recipe

Follow these steps to create a restaurant-quality Palak Paneer in your own kitchen:

**Step 1: Prepare the Spinach**
Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the washed spinach leaves and blanch for just 1-2 minutes until they wilt and turn bright green. Immediately drain the spinach and transfer it to a bowl of ice-cold water (this "shocks" the spinach, preserving its vibrant color). Once cooled, squeeze out excess water and blend the spinach into a smooth purée using a blender or food processor. Set aside.

**Step 2: Prepare the Paneer**
Cut the paneer into desired cubes. You can use it as is, or for a slightly firmer texture and golden crust, lightly pan-fry or shallow-fry the paneer cubes in a little ghee or oil until golden brown on all sides. If frying, immediately transfer the fried paneer to a bowl of warm water for 10 minutes to keep it soft. Drain before adding to the curry.

**Step 3: Sauté Aromatics**
Heat ghee or oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai over medium heat. Once hot, add the cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent and light golden brown.

**Step 4: Add Ginger, Garlic, and Chillies**
Add the ginger-garlic paste and chopped green chillies. Sauté for another minute until the raw smell disappears.

**Step 5: Introduce Spices and Tomatoes (Optional)**
Reduce the heat to low. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and red chilli powder. Stir continuously for 30 seconds to cook the spices, being careful not to burn them. If using, add the chopped or puréed tomatoes and cook until they soften and the oil starts to separate from the masala (this indicates the tomatoes are well cooked).

**Step 6: Incorporate Spinach Purée**
Add the prepared spinach purée to the pan. Mix well with the मसाला (spice mixture). Add salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cooking for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld. Be cautious as the spinach mixture can splatter.

**Step 7: Add Paneer and Adjust Consistency**
Gently fold in the paneer cubes. Add 1/4 to 1/2 cup of water or cream to achieve your desired consistency. If using cream, it will add richness and a smoother texture. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes, allowing the paneer to absorb the flavors, but avoid overcooking the paneer, which can make it rubbery.

**Step 8: Finish and Garnish**
Stir in the garam masala. Taste and adjust salt if necessary. Remove from heat. If desired, swirl in a tablespoon of fresh cream and garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.

## Tips for the Best Palak Paneer

*   **Blanching is Key:** Don't skip the blanching and ice bath step for the spinach. It locks in the vibrant green color and prevents the spinach from turning dull or bitter.
*   **Fresh Paneer:** While store-bought paneer works, fresh, homemade paneer has an unparalleled soft texture.
*   **Don't Overcook Spinach:** Overcooking spinach can lead to nutrient loss and a less appealing color and taste.
*   **Right Consistency:** Adjust the amount of water or cream to get your preferred gravy consistency. Some prefer it thick, others a bit thinner.
*   **Ghee for Flavor:** Using ghee instead of oil for tempering the spices and sautéing the aromatics imparts a distinct, rich, and authentic flavor to the Palak Paneer.
*   **Gentle Handling of Paneer:** Paneer is delicate. Add it towards the end and simmer gently to prevent it from becoming tough or crumbly.

## Serving Suggestions for Palak Paneer

Palak Paneer is incredibly versatile and pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice dishes.

*   **Indian Breads:** Serve hot with freshly made naan, roti, chapati, or paratha for scooping up the luscious gravy.
*   **Rice:** It's a fantastic accompaniment to plain steamed basmati rice or aromatic jeera rice (cumin rice).
*   **Side Dishes:** Complete your meal with a side of cooling raita (yogurt dip), a simple dal (lentil soup), or a fresh kachumber salad.
*   **Meal Idea:** For a complete Indian thali experience, serve Palak Paneer alongside dal makhani, a vegetable sabzi, rice, bread, and a sweet dish.

## Palak Paneer Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Palak Paneer is perfect as is, you can explore several variations to suit dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegan Palak Tofu:** For a vegan version, substitute paneer with firm or extra-firm tofu, pressed and cut into cubes. You can also use cashew cream or coconut milk instead of dairy cream for richness.
*   **Creaminess Boost:** For an extra creamy texture without dairy cream, add a tablespoon of cashew paste (cashews soaked in warm water and ground into a paste) or almond paste to the spinach purée.
*   **Spicier Kick:** Increase the number of green chillies or add a pinch of red chilli flakes for a hotter version.
*   **Smoky Flavor (Dhungar):** For an authentic smoky touch, place a small, heated piece of charcoal in a small metal bowl in the center of the finished curry. Drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over the charcoal, cover immediately, and let it infuse for 5-7 minutes before serving.
*   **Other Greens:** While spinach is traditional, you can experiment with other leafy greens like mustard greens (sarson) or fenugreek leaves (methi) for a different flavor profile.
*   **Added Vegetables:** Occasionally, peas (matar) or corn are added along with the paneer for extra texture and sweetness.

Palak Paneer is more than just a dish; it’s a testament to the rich culinary heritage of India, offering comfort, nutrition, and an explosion of delightful flavors in every bite. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a kitchen novice, this recipe provides a clear path to mastering a truly iconic Indian vegetarian dish. Enjoy the process, and savor the delicious results!
